title: Getting Started with StatsPackage
text: Use ci_mu() one-sample two-sample mean intervals: Use ci_p() one-sample exact intervals two-sample difference intervals:
code: ci_mu(xbar = 12.4, n = 15, s = 3.2, quiet = TRUE) ci_mu(xbar = c(65, 63), n = c(40, 38), s = c(3.0, 2.5), method = "welch", quiet = TRUE) ci_p(x = 56, n = 100, quiet = TRUE) ci_p(x = c(42, 30), n = c(100, 100), quiet = TRUE)

title: Getting Started with StatsPackage
text: p_test() supports one-sample exact two-sample z tests. Variance-family tests use tail-based two-sided rejection regions:
code: p_test(x = 21, n = 100, p0 = 0.20, quiet = TRUE) p_test(x = c(35, 24), n = c(100, 100), p0 = 0, quiet = TRUE) var_test_chisq(s = 4.2, n = 12, sigma0 = 5, alternative = "two.sided", quiet = TRUE)

title: Getting Started with StatsPackage
code: obs <- c(18, 35, 31, 16) chisq_gof_probs(observed = obs, p = c(0.2, 0.3, 0.3, 0.2), quiet = TRUE) tab <- matrix(c(22, 18, 14, 26), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE) chisq_table(tab, type = "independence", quiet = TRUE) table_props(tab, margin = "row", quiet = TRUE)

title: Getting Started with StatsPackage
code: power_z_mu(mu_a = 103, mu0 = 100, sigma = 10, n = 64, quiet = TRUE) n_required_z_mu(mu_a = 103, mu0 = 100, sigma = 10, beta_target = 0.1, quiet = TRUE)

title: Getting Started with StatsPackage

what: Chapter 14 chi-square and contingency-table functions — chisq_functions
title: Chapter 14 chi-square and contingency-table functions — chisq_functions
text: chisq_gof_probs() performs one-way chi-square goodness--fit test specified category probabilities.

title: Chapter 14 chi-square and contingency-table functions — chisq_functions
code: chisq_gof_probs(   observed,   p = NULL,   labels = NULL,   min_expected = 5,   alpha = 0.05,   digits = 4,   quiet = FALSE )  chisq_gof_dist(   x = NULL,   observed = NULL,   dist = c("exp", "norm", "pois"),   k = NULL,   breaks = NULL,   params = NULL,   estimate = FALSE,   params_estimated = FALSE,   min_expected = 5,   alpha = 0.05,   digits = 4,   quiet = FALSE )  chisq_table(   observed,   type = c("independence", "homogeneity"),   correct = FALSE,   min_expected = 5,   alpha = 0.05,   digits = 4,   quiet = FALSE )  table_props(   observed,   margin = c("row", "col", "overall"),   digits = 4,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Chapter 14 chi-square and contingency-table functions — chisq_functions
text: observed Observed counts. chisq_gof_probs() grouped chisq_gof_dist() inputs, use nonnegative integer vector. chisq_table() table_props(), use nonnegative two-way table matrix. p Probabilities null chisq_gof_probs(). NULL, equal probabilities assumed. labels Optional category labels one-way grouped inputs. min_expected Minimum expected count threshold used warnings , implemented, class combining. alpha Significance level (0, 1). digits Integer number decimal places used printed output. quiet Logical; TRUE, suppress printed output. x Raw sample data chisq_gof_dist(). dist Distribution chisq_gof_dist(): "pois", "norm", "exp". k Number classes used raw-data GOF tests breaks supplied. breaks Class boundaries grouped chisq_gof_dist() input. params Named list distribution parameters parameters estimated raw data. estimate Logical; TRUE, estimate parameters raw data applicable. params_estimated Logical; grouped GOF inputs, whether supplied parameters count degrees freedom. type chisq_table(), whether interpretation "independence" "homogeneity". correct Logical; TRUE, apply Yates correction 2 x 2 case chisq_table(). margin table_props(), one "row", "col", "overall".

title: Chapter 14 chi-square and contingency-table functions — chisq_functions
text: chi-square test functions, classed htest_result object statistic, df, p-value, critical value, rejection region, decision, diagnostic outputs observed counts, expected counts, contributions, residuals. chisq_table(), standardized residuals also returned. table_props(), classed table_props_result object returned original table requested proportions.

title: Chapter 14 chi-square and contingency-table functions — chisq_functions
text: chisq_gof_dist() performs chi-square goodness--fit test distribution, supporting least Poisson, normal, exponential models. chisq_table() performs chi-square test independence homogeneity r x c contingency table. table_props() computes descriptive row, column, overall proportions contingency table.

title: Chapter 14 chi-square and contingency-table functions — chisq_functions
text: chisq_gof_probs(): One-way chi-square GOF test specified probabilities. chisq_gof_dist(): Chi-square GOF test distribution. chisq_table(): Chi-square test contingency table. table_props(): Descriptive proportions contingency table.

title: Confidence interval functions — ci_functions
text: Computes confidence intervals means, proportions, variances, exponential rate parameter.

title: Confidence interval functions — ci_functions
code: ci_mu(   xbar,   n,   s = NULL,   sigma = NULL,   conf.level = 0.95,   paired = FALSE,   side = c("two.sided", "lower", "upper"),   method = c("welch", "pooled", "z"),   digits = 4,   quiet = FALSE )  ci_p(x, n, conf.level = 0.95, exact_1s = TRUE, digits = 4, quiet = FALSE)  ci_var(s, n, conf.level = 0.95, digits = 4, quiet = FALSE)  ci_lambda_exp(Sum, n, conf.level = 0.95, digits = 4, quiet = FALSE)

title: Confidence interval functions — ci_functions
text: xbar Numeric vector sample means paired-difference mean summaries. n Integer sample size vector sample sizes. s Sample standard deviation input sigma unknown. sigma Known population standard deviation input. conf.level Confidence level (0, 1). paired Logical; ci_mu(), whether supplied summaries paired differences. side Interval side specification: "two.sided", "lower", "upper". method Method two-sample sigma-unknown mean interval. digits Integer number decimal places used printed output. quiet Logical; TRUE, suppress printed output. x Count input proportion, variance, exponential-rate intervals. exact_1s Logical; ci_p(), whether use exact one-sided bounds. Sum Sum observations exponential-rate interval.

title: Confidence interval functions — ci_functions
text: functions return classed result objects unrounded stored values optional printed summaries controlled digits quiet.

title: Confidence interval functions — ci_functions
text: ci_mu(): Confidence interval mean mean difference. ci_p(): Confidence interval proportion difference proportions. ci_var(): Confidence interval variance variance ratio. ci_lambda_exp(): Confidence interval exponential rate parameter.

title: Hypothesis-testing functions — hypothesis_test_functions
text: Performs common one-sample two-sample hypothesis tests means, proportions, variances.

title: Hypothesis-testing functions — hypothesis_test_functions
code: z_test_mu(   xbar,   mu0,   sigma = NULL,   n,   alpha = 0.05,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),   digits = 4,   quiet = FALSE,   s = NULL )  t_test_mu(   xbar,   mu0,   s,   n,   alpha = 0.05,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),   var.equal = FALSE,   digits = 4,   quiet = FALSE,   paired = FALSE )  p_test(   x,   n,   p0,   alpha = 0.05,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),   digits = 4,   quiet = FALSE,   check_npq = TRUE,   pooled = NULL,   continuity = FALSE )  var_test_chisq(   s,   n,   sigma0 = NULL,   ratio0 = 1,   alpha = 0.05,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),   digits = 4,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Hypothesis-testing functions — hypothesis_test_functions
text: xbar Numeric vector sample means summary statistics. mu0 Null-hypothesis value mean mean difference. sigma Known population standard deviation input. n Integer sample size vector sample sizes. alpha Significance level (0, 1). alternative Alternative hypothesis direction: "two.sided", "less", "greater". digits Integer number decimal places used printed output. quiet Logical; TRUE, suppress printed output. s Sample standard deviation input sigma unknown. var.equal Logical; t_test_mu(), whether use pooled-variance test. paired Logical; t_test_mu(), whether supplied summaries paired differences. x Count input proportion variance tests. p0 Null-hypothesis proportion value difference proportions. check_npq Logical; whether report normal-approximation adequacy checks proportion tests. pooled Logical; two-sample proportion tests, whether use pooled standard errors. continuity Logical. TRUE, apply continuity correction two-sample z test proportions. Ignored one-sample exact tests. sigma0 Null-hypothesis standard deviation chi-square variance test. ratio0 Null-hypothesis variance ratio F test.

title: Hypothesis-testing functions — hypothesis_test_functions
text: functions return classed result objects unrounded stored values optional printed summaries controlled digits quiet.

title: Hypothesis-testing functions — hypothesis_test_functions
text: z_test_mu(): Z test mean difference means known sigma. t_test_mu(): t test mean difference means. p_test(): Hypothesis test one two proportions. var_test_chisq(): Chi-square F test variances.

what: Sample-size functions for target power — n_required_functions
title: Sample-size functions for target power — n_required_functions
text: Determines smallest sample size achieves requested target power common hypothesis tests.

title: Sample-size functions for target power — n_required_functions
code: n_required_from_power(   power_at_n,   target_power,   n_min = 1L,   n_max = 1000000L,   digits = 4,   quiet = FALSE )  n_required_z_mu(   mu_a,   mu0,   sigma,   alpha = 0.05,   beta_target = 0.1,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),   n_min = 1L,   n_max = 1000000L,   n_ratio = 1,   digits = 4,   quiet = FALSE )  n_required_t_mu(   mu_a,   mu0,   sigma_true,   alpha = 0.05,   beta_target = 0.1,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),   n_min = 2L,   n_max = 1000000L,   paired = FALSE,   method = c("welch", "pooled"),   n_ratio = 1,   digits = 4,   quiet = FALSE )  n_required_p_z(   p_a,   p0,   alpha = 0.05,   beta_target = 0.1,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),   n_min = 5L,   n_max = 10000000L,   pooled = NULL,   continuity = FALSE,   n_ratio = 1,   digits = 4,   quiet = FALSE )  n_required_var_chisq(   sigma_a,   sigma0,   alpha = 0.05,   beta_target = 0.1,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),   n_min = 2L,   n_max = 1000000L,   digits = 4,   quiet = FALSE )  n_required_var_ratio_F(   sigma_a,   ratio0 = 1,   alpha = 0.05,   beta_target = 0.1,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),   n_min = 2L,   n_max = 1000000L,   n_ratio = 1,   digits = 4,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Sample-size functions for target power — n_required_functions
text: power_at_n Function returning power proposed integer sample size. target_power Target power (0, 1). n_min Minimum integer sample size considered search. n_max Maximum integer sample size considered search. digits Integer number decimal places used printed output. quiet Logical; TRUE, suppress printed output. mu_a True mean value vector true means alternative. mu0 Null-hypothesis mean value mean-difference value. sigma Known population standard deviation input. alpha Significance level (0, 1). beta_target Target type II error (0, 1). alternative Alternative hypothesis direction: "two.sided", "less", "greater". n_ratio Allocation ratio n2 / n1 two-sample designs. sigma_true True population standard deviation input used t-test planning. paired Logical; n_required_t_mu(), whether supplied summaries paired differences. method Method two-sample sigma-unknown t-test planning calculation. p_a True proportion value vector true proportions alternative. p0 Null-hypothesis proportion value difference proportions. pooled Logical; two-sample proportion planning, whether use pooled standard errors. continuity Logical; whether apply continuity correction supported. sigma_a True standard deviation value vector alternative. sigma0 Null-hypothesis standard deviation chi-square variance test. ratio0 Null-hypothesis variance ratio F test.

title: Sample-size functions for target power — n_required_functions
text: functions return classed result objects unrounded stored values optional printed summaries controlled digits quiet.

title: Sample-size functions for target power — n_required_functions
text: n_required_from_power(): Generic integer sample-size solver based power function. n_required_z_mu(): Required sample size z tests means known sigma. n_required_t_mu(): Required sample size t tests means. n_required_p_z(): Required sample size z tests proportions. n_required_var_chisq(): Required sample size one-sample chi-square tests variance. n_required_var_ratio_F(): Required sample size two-sample F tests variance ratio.

title: Power functions — power_functions
text: Computes power type II error common tests means, proportions, variances.

title: Power functions — power_functions
code: power_z_mu(   mu_a,   mu0,   sigma,   n,   alpha = 0.05,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),   digits = 4,   quiet = FALSE )  power_t_mu(   mu_a,   mu0,   sigma_true,   n,   alpha = 0.05,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),   paired = FALSE,   method = c("welch", "pooled"),   digits = 4,   quiet = FALSE )  power_p_z(   p_a,   p0,   n,   alpha = 0.05,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),   digits = 4,   quiet = FALSE,   pooled = NULL,   continuity = FALSE )  power_var_chisq(   sigma_a,   sigma0,   n,   alpha = 0.05,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),   digits = 4,   quiet = FALSE )  power_var_ratio_F(   sigma_a,   ratio0 = 1,   n,   alpha = 0.05,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),   digits = 4,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Power functions — power_functions
text: mu_a True mean value vector true means alternative. mu0 Null-hypothesis mean value mean-difference value. sigma Known population standard deviation input. n Integer sample size vector sample sizes. alpha Significance level (0, 1). alternative Alternative hypothesis direction: "two.sided", "less", "greater". digits Integer number decimal places used printed output. quiet Logical; TRUE, suppress printed output. sigma_true True population standard deviation input used t-test power calculations. paired Logical; power_t_mu(), whether supplied summaries paired differences. method Method two-sample sigma-unknown t-test power calculation. p_a True proportion value vector true proportions alternative. p0 Null-hypothesis proportion value difference proportions. pooled Logical; two-sample proportion power, whether use pooled standard errors. continuity Logical; whether apply continuity correction supported. sigma_a True standard deviation value vector alternative. sigma0 Null-hypothesis standard deviation chi-square variance test. ratio0 Null-hypothesis variance ratio F test.

title: Power functions — power_functions
text: functions return classed result objects unrounded stored values optional printed summaries controlled digits quiet.

title: Power functions — power_functions
text: power_z_mu(): Power z tests means known sigma. power_t_mu(): Power t tests means. power_p_z(): Power z tests proportions. power_var_chisq(): Power one-sample chi-square tests variance. power_var_ratio_F(): Power two-sample F tests variance ratio.

title: Descriptive shape functions — shape_functions
text: Computes skewness kurtosis measures optional small-sample adjustments plain-language interpretations.

title: Descriptive shape functions — shape_functions
code: skew(   x,   method = c("moment", "adjusted"),   na_rm = FALSE,   digits = 4,   quiet = FALSE )  kurt(   x,   method = c("moment", "adjusted"),   excess = FALSE,   na_rm = FALSE,   digits = 4,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Descriptive shape functions — shape_functions
text: x Numeric sample vector. method Estimator choice shape measure. na_rm Logical; TRUE, remove missing values computing measure. digits Integer number decimal places used printed output. quiet Logical; TRUE, suppress printed output. excess Logical; kurt(), whether report excess kurtosis instead Pearson kurtosis.

title: Descriptive shape functions — shape_functions
text: functions return classed result objects unrounded stored values optional printed summaries controlled digits quiet.

title: Descriptive shape functions — shape_functions
text: skew(): Sample skewness measure interpretation. kurt(): Sample kurtosis measure interpretation.

title: Confidence-interval width planning functions — width_plan_functions
text: Determines sample size required achieve target total confidence interval width selected interval procedures.

title: Confidence-interval width planning functions — width_plan_functions
code: n_width_mu_z(w, sigma, conf.level = 0.95, digits = 4, quiet = FALSE)  n_width_p_wald(   w,   conf.level = 0.95,   p = 0.5,   worst_case = TRUE,   digits = 4,   quiet = FALSE )  n_width_mu_t(   w,   s,   conf.level = 0.95,   n_start = NULL,   max_iter = 100,   digits = 4,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Confidence-interval width planning functions — width_plan_functions
text: w Target total confidence-interval width. sigma Known population standard deviation input. conf.level Confidence level (0, 1). digits Integer number decimal places used printed output. quiet Logical; TRUE, suppress printed output. p Planning value proportion. worst_case Logical; TRUE, use worst-case proportion value. s Sample standard deviation used t-based width planning. n_start Starting sample size iterative width planning. max_iter Maximum number search iterations.

title: Confidence-interval width planning functions — width_plan_functions
text: functions return classed result objects unrounded stored values optional printed summaries controlled digits quiet.

title: Confidence-interval width planning functions — width_plan_functions
text: n_width_mu_z(): Required sample size z interval mean. n_width_p_wald(): Required sample size Wald interval proportion. n_width_mu_t(): Required sample size t interval mean.

title: StatsPackage 0.1.2
text: Fix pkgdown metadata include deployed site URL DESCRIPTION. Add ignore rules generated pkgdown site artifacts. Keep release automation package metadata aligned next tag.

title: StatsPackage 0.1.1
text: Align package version post-0.1.0 release maintenance. Add initial user vignette core workflows. Add pkgdown configuration generated reference article site. Clarify release/QA automation CI expectations repository documentation.

title: StatsPackage 0.1.0
text: First tagged stable release package-quality toolkit. Confidence interval, hypothesis testing, power, required-sample-size families available. Categorical-data functions added (chisq_gof_probs(), chisq_gof_dist(), chisq_table(), table_props()). Regression-tested behavior cheat-sheet-based QA workflow established.
